instruction: The support queue project in ~/Desktop/support_queue/ is being handed over. Read HANDOFF.json to get the new queueName, contactEmail, dashboardUrl, docsUrl, and VS Code preferences. Open the docsUrl from the handoff in Chrome and keep it open while you work. Then use VS Code to update queue_config.json so queueName, contactEmail, and dashboardUrl match the handoff; create a backup named queue_config.json.bak; repair the status line builder so the provided tests pass; create RUNBOOK.md whose first line is '# <queueName>' and whose second line is 'Dashboard: <dashboardUrl>'; and apply the tabSize and ruler from the handoff to VS Code User Settings.
source: 现实场景构造来源：支持队列工具交接时常见的“更新 queue_config、修状态行脚本、补 runbook、同步文档与编辑器偏好”运维 handoff 流程

parameters.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path
base = Path('/home/user/Desktop/support_queue')
base.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
json.dump({'queueName': 'Support Escalations', 'contactEmail': 'support-ops@example.com', 'dashboardUrl': 'https://status.example.com/support-queue', 'docsUrl': 'https://docs.python.org/3/library/csv.html', 'tabSize': 4, 'ruler': 100}, open(base / 'HANDOFF.json', 'w'), indent=2)
json.dump({'queueName': 'Legacy Queue', 'contactEmail': 'legacy@example.com', 'dashboardUrl': 'https://status.example.com/legacy'}, open(base / 'queue_config.json', 'w'), indent=2)
(base / 'helpers.py').write_text("""def normalize_level(level):
    return level.lower()
""")
(base / 'build_status_line.py').write_text("""from helpers import normalize_level

def build_status_line(queue, level):
    return f'{queue}: {normalize_level(level)}'
""")
(base / 'test.py').write_text("""from build_status_line import build_status_line

def test():
    assert build_status_line('refunds', 'HIGH') == 'refunds [HIGH]'
""")
PY
